question:
Will Chipotle say "Chipotle Lane / Chipotlane" during earnings call?

Description: This market will resolve based on the next earnings announcement of Chipotle currently scheduled to take place on October 29, 2025 at 5:00 PM EDT.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone during this event. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A Q&A event will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this event is definitely cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be audio of the event.

evidences.title: CHIPOTLE ANNOUNCES FIRST QUARTER 2024 RESULTS - Apr 24, 2024
evidences.content: Results for the three months ended March 31, 2024: Total revenue in the first quarter was $2.7 billion, an increase of 14.1% compared to the first quarter of 2023. The increase in total revenue was driven by new restaurant openings and a 7.0% increase in comparable restaurant sales due to higher transactions of 5.4% and a 1.6% increase in average check. Digital sales represented 36.5% of total food and beverage revenue. We opened 47 new restaurants during the first quarter with 43 locations including a Chipotlane. These formats continue to perform well and are helping enhance guest access and convenience, as well as increase new restaurant sales, margins, and returns. [...] Diluted earnings per share was $13.01, a 23.9% increase from $10.50. Adjusted diluted earnings per share, which excluded a $0.36 after-tax impact from an increase in legal reserves, was $13.371, a 27.3% increase from $10.501. Opened 47 new restaurants with 43 locations including a Chipotlane [...] Food, beverage and packaging costs in the first quarter were 28.8% of total revenue, a decrease of about 40 basis points compared to the first quarter of 2023. The decrease was primarily due to menu price increases...

evidences.title: Chipotle Mexican Grill to announce third quarter results
evidences.content: Chipotle said it would host its next quarterly results conference call in the late afternoon Eastern time to discuss third quarter financial results and provide a business update. The company said a press release with financial results would be issued shortly before the call, and that the event would be available by phone and webcast with an archived replay afterward.

evidences.title: Why Chipotle’s 2025 Outlook Looks Conservative – Placer.ai Blog
evidences.content: Chipotle's “Chipotlane” format stores—which include a dedicated drive-thru lanes for digital order pickups—has significantly enhanced operational efficiency. According to management, Chipotlane location stores often see transactions completed in less than a minute, which compares favorably to traditional QSR drive-thru times. This swift service has led to a 10%-15% increase in sales at Chipotlane-equipped locations compared to traditional formats. Chipotle now has more than 1,000 Chipotlane locations, with plans to include this feature in the majority of new restaurants, aiming for an annual unit growth of 8% to 10%. [...] Overall, while 2025 presents a challenging landscape for the restaurant industry, Chipotle appears well-positioned to navigate these headwinds and potentially exceed its growth expectations. The company’s proven track record of successful menu innovations, along with the promising early results of Honey Chicken, demonstrate its ability to resonate with consumers. Additionally, Chipotle's strategic expansion into smaller markets and the continued rollout of Chipotlane locations are key drivers that could boost visitation and operational efficiency. Despite a di...

evidences.title: cmg-20231231x10k
evidences.content: Our digital platform continues to be a strategic driver of our growth. In recent years, we have significantly upgraded our capabilities by digitizing our restaurant kitchens, expanding our partnerships with third-party delivery services and building more Chipotlanes, which is our drive through format for customer pick-up of digital orders. Digital sales represent food and beverage revenue generated through the Chipotle website, Chipotle app or third-party delivery aggregators and include revenue deferrals associated with Chipotle Rewards. Digital sales represented 37.4% of food and beverage revenue in 2023, compared to 39.4% of food and beverage revenue in 2022. We have made digital ordering convenient with enhancements to the Chipotle app and website, such as customization, contactless [...] At Chipotle, our vision is to cultivate an environment where our employees can thrive, pursue their passion and become lifelong leaders. We believe in investing and supporting our people because they are our most important asset and give us a competitive advantage in our business. As of December 3 1, 2023, Chipotle employed 116,068 people worldwide and 1,088 contract workers. Of our employe...

evidences.title: Management - Chipotle Mexican Grill
evidences.content: Adam Rymer Chief Financial Officer Adam Rymer is the Chief Financial Officer for Chipotle Mexican Grill, responsible for corporate and field financial planning and analysis, as well as the financial reporting, tax, treasury and investor relations functions. Mr. Rymer is a member of the Executive Leadership Team, reporting to the Chief Executive Officer. He previously served as Vice President of Finance and has held a variety of positions in Finance and People Experience since joining the organization in 2009. Additionally, he is the Treasurer of the Chipotle Cultivate Foundation. In 2025, Mr. Rymer was named Extel All America Best CFO for the restaurant sector. [...] Prior to Chipotle, Mr. Boatwright spent 18 years with Arby’s Restaurant Group in various leadership positions. He most recently served as the Senior Vice President of Operations, where he was responsible for the performance of over 1,700 Arby’s restaurants in numerous states. Mr. Boatwright is a member of Business Roundtable, Business Council, and National Restaurant Association’s Fast Casual Industry Council. He holds a Master of Business Administration from the J. Mack Robinson College of Business at Georgia State...

evidences.title: Chipotle discusses targeting higher-income customers and modest menu price increases
evidences.content: Chipotle management said the company sees confidence in higher-income core users and plans modest menu price increases of 1–2% while continuing promotions and other initiatives to re-engage younger and lower-income customers.

evidences.title: Chipotle Strategy and Business Model
evidences.content: Accelerating unit growth: Management has continued to target hundreds of new restaurant openings annually, with a large majority of new units including a Chipotlane. This matters because the company still sees substantial whitespace in North America and views new restaurant development as its clearest long-term growth lever. Increasing throughput: Chipotle has repeatedly emphasized serving more guests during peak periods. That means better staffing, clearer line roles, improved kitchen sequencing, and tighter execution across both front-line and digital orders. Throughput is strategically important because it raises sales without requiring a full menu redesign. [...] North America whitespace strategy: Build a fact-based market prioritization model for the path toward a much larger North American restaurant base, including trade-area screening and cannibalization analysis. Chipotlane economics and format optimization: Assess where Chipotlanes create the strongest returns, and refine the site-selection criteria for suburban, urban, and hybrid formats. Peak-hour throughput diagnostic: Map line bottlenecks across front counter and digital make-lines, then redesign labor deployment,...

evidences.content: Our total capital expenditures for 2023 were $560.7 million. In 2024, we expect to incur about $635.0 million in total capital expenditures. We expect approximately $430.0 million in capital expenditures related to our construction of new restaurants, before any reductions for landlord reimbursements. For new restaurants to be opened in 2024, we anticipate average development costs will remain consistent with 2023.
